commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: fix(ETC-534): recently added styles check race condition clearing styles (#268)
commits.body:
Context:
https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/class-parsing-gone-haywire

I am fairly certain that this will fix the issue. Instead of batching by
microtask we now batch by macrotask. This should ensure the styles
aren't accidentally cleared, and should "survive" svelte effect flushes.

As i was never able to replicate myself i sent a dev version to zack
pyle to confirm the fix. Once he gets back to me and confirms the fix
works I'll merge this.

commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: improvement: ETC-515 greatly reduce load in initial builder open (#267)
commits.body:
Instead of having a broken n+1 problem with templates we now only load
templates on demand (when actually switching to the templates).

I initially tested just including the complete template data in the
template list but this causes a bunch of OOM issues since our dynamic
data resolving is pretty memory intensive.

Previous appraoch:

1. Fetch templates list
2. Immediatly fetch complete template data of ALL templates in the list
3. On top of that every template load additionally loaded a bunch of
preview posts for said template, including dynamic data
Turns out due to that, the load on PHP was actually MUCH greater than
initially anticipated.

New approach
1. Fetch template list
2. Only fetch specific template when "Edit Template" is clicked
3. Since the preview data is also only loaded on template load, this is
deferred too

ETC-550.title: Alpine JS specifc attribtues stripped on frontend
ETC-550.description:
Currently we have a bug that alpine js specifc attributes are stripped from rendering on the frontend. Even when things are rendering via shortcodes and are never *touched* by etch.
Reason for this being that `remove_wp_site_blocks_wrapper` is faulty. We utilize the PHP native DOMDocument which seems to strip out @click and similar attributes (even though they are theoretically valid in the HTML spec)

Reason for this ticket: [https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/pie-calendar-plugin-not-working-in-etch#comment_wrapper_99028888](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/pie-calendar-plugin-not-working-in-etch#comment_wrapper_99028888>)

AC:
User can use @click and other apline js attributes and they render fine in the frontend and builder
Shortcodes rendering alpine js (or similar) attributes will render fine in the frontend and builder

ETC-538.title: Reload Javascript upstream on child mounts
ETC-538.description:
Currently when a child element mount the javascript upstream does not reload, which can cause annoying issue when parent JS tries to select a child element. This is fixed by reloading the page.

To not have to reload the builder every time, a reloading of upstream JS on child mounts, would fix the issue.

AC:

* Adding a child causes parent JS to reload
* JS selecting a child will automatically be able to select the new child without havign to reload a page.

ETC-541.title: Component groups cause error when adding classes to parent elements
ETC-541.description:
## [https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/components-disappearing-when-editing-class](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/components-disappearing-when-editing-class>)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create component
2. Add groups
3. Add pops to groups
4. Try adding a new class to a parent container

---

### Environment

Plugin Version: 1.4.5

Theme Version: 0.0.7

WordPress Version: 6.9.4

Machine: Mac

Browser: Chromium

---

### Magic Login

---

### Attachments

[https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/components-disappearing-when-editing-class](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/components-disappearing-when-editing-class>)

### Reporter

Email: [denis.schischka@icloud.com](<mailto:denis.schischka@icloud.com>)

ETC-540.title: Setting to allow behaviour change for pasting.
ETC-540.description:
Currently when pasting styles and components will not be overwritten if there is a selector or component key conflict. To allow a path for this a simple way to do this would be to add a setting "Overwrite styles and components on paste collision".

AC:

* When toggled on if there is a style collision the style is overwritten with the data from the paste
* When toggled on if there is a component key collision the component is overwritten with the data from the paste
* When toggled off the existing style is preserved
* When toggled off the existing component is preserved

ETC-539.title: Component Instance props not properly copy pasted

ETC-534.title: Partial class fix breaks pasting in HTML Editor

ETC-532.title: Add small small error hint on unbalanced brackets
ETC-532.description:
One of the most reoccuring issues (it feels like at least once a week), is people saving css with unbalanced curly brackets. It's actually very easy to add a codemirror extension that adds a small error.
This will highlight the issue and make it easier for users to notice and fix.

This ticket is an important personal concern for me since i'm tired of writing the same exact commetn once a week.

AC:
When writing CSS and adding and extra curly brace, or a missing curly brace. The user gets a short warning notifiying him of the issue.

ETC-515.title: Editor load fires 30+ individual REST API requests, causing 15-20s load on InstaWP
ETC-515.description:
## Summary

When the Etch editor loads (`?etch=magic&post_id=...`), it fires \~30+ individual `GET /wp-json/etch-api/post/{id}` requests — one per post/template. On InstaWP, these requests queue up and the builder takes 15–20 seconds to become usable. The same sites load their frontend in under 100ms.

A test website on this infrastructure is available for reproduction:

* [https://eatch-test-prod.instawp.site](<https://eatch-test-prod.instawp.site>)
* Username: `reyilatezi7185`
* Password: `cbDZnvFlRXOQe9H7qEoA`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When the user opens up the etch builder it should only fetch the actually needed template/post data.
- [ ] The user can still switch back and forth between posts as usual
- [ ] All available templates and posts will still be listed in the Templates panel and the content hub
